criteria.summary: Before the change, the resolved Mini App URL was local HTTP (`http://localhost:3001/`) and Telegram launch was disabled/unsafe.
Selected `https://happytg.gerta.crazedns.ru/miniapp` via `HAPPYTG_MINIAPP_URL`; local Mini App URL remains diagnostics-only.
After the external edge/TLS route was fixed, `https://happytg.gerta.crazedns.ru/miniapp` returned HTTP 200. Port 8443 remains unselected and unavailable.
Repo Caddy route is recorded; host-run BaseDeploy Caddy route was added and reloaded with upstream `127.0.0.1:3007`.
`/ready` reports `miniAppLaunch.status=ready` and the selected public HTTPS URL after bot restart.
`/start`, `/menu`, session, detail, and diff surfaces include Telegram `web_app` buttons using the selected public HTTPS URL; no localhost URL is present.
Dry-run passed with Caddy HTTP 200 and the public HTTPS MenuButtonWebApp payload.
Actual menu set passed and Telegram accepted the HappyTG Mini App menu button.
Bot/bootstrap tests, typecheck, lint, full test, and build passed after the Windows Telegram API fallback code change.
`pnpm happytg task validate --repo . --task HTG-2026-04-23-enable-miniapp-launch-buttons` passed, and the fresh verifier pass confirms completion.
